response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned generalist with strong software craftsmanship fundamentals and genuine experience in developer tooling, but his technical profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His primary languages are TypeScript, DLang, and Node.js; Java appears only briefly in early career work (robotic arms, mobile orchestration at Altom) and is listed as a skill without depth or recency. There is no evidence of Kotlin usage, JVM internals knowledge, Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate experience, or IDE subsystem development in a JVM context — all of which are core requirements for this position.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) consistently applied across roles
Demonstrable developer tooling experience including an IDE-adjacent role at National Instruments on NI LabWindows/CVI and recent MCP/agentic tooling work
Experience building and maintaining large-scale, high-throughput distributed systems (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin expertise; current and recent roles are exclusively TypeScript/Node.js
Zero Spring ecosystem experience — Spring, Quarkus, and Hibernate are entirely absent from the resume
No JVM internals or JVM-specific language/IDE tooling background, which is the core technical domain of the role

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and craftsmanship track record
